description: External Communications — Discord bridge, Teams/Slack channel email relay

tasks.title: Channel Email Relay (Teams/Slack)
tasks.prompt:
Implement Channel Email Relay (M6.5) as specified in SNAPP-TRD.md WU-6.5.

Add to Network Service (or create separate endpoints):

  POST /api/networks/{netId}/relays — add channel relay (steward only). Encrypt channel email. Send verification email to channel. Store RELAY# item with IsVerified=false.
  POST /api/networks/{netId}/relays/{channelId}/verify?code={code} — verify channel (validates code, sets IsVerified=true, sends confirmation)
  GET /api/networks/{netId}/relays — list relays (steward only, decrypt emails for display)
  PUT /api/networks/{netId}/relays/{channelId} — update relay types/label
  DELETE /api/networks/{netId}/relays/{channelId} — remove relay

Content relay logic (in Content Service or via event):
  After post/milestone creation: check for verified relays on the network, send formatted HTML email to channel address. Fire-and-forget (don't block post creation).

Digest relay: after daily digest run, send network activity summary to channels with 'digest' relay type.

Razor email templates for channel relay (simple HTML, inline CSS, under 25KB).

For testing: configure a relay pointing at a Papercut email address. Verify relay emails arrive in Papercut.

Integration tests: add relay, verify, create post, verify relay email in Papercut, delete relay.
tasks.done_when: Channel relay CRUD works. Verification flow works. Posts relay to channel email via Papercut. Tests pass.

tasks.title: Channel Relay UI
tasks.prompt:
Add channel relay management to Network Settings using MudBlazor.

Update Pages/Network/Settings.razor, add new MudTab 'Channels':

1. MudList of connected channels: label, MudChip platform (Teams/Slack/Other), MudChip status (Verified/Pending), relay types
2. MudButton 'Add Channel' → MudDialog:
   - MudSelect Platform (Teams/Slack/Other)
   - MudTextField Channel Email
   - MudTextField Label
   - MudCheckBox group: relay types (Posts/Milestones/Digest)
   - Instructions panel: MudExpansionPanel 'How to find your channel email' with platform-specific steps
3. Per-channel: MudIconButton Edit (relay types), MudIconButton Delete
4. Pending channels: MudAlert 'Waiting for verification — check your channel for the verification email'

bUnit tests.
tasks.done_when: Channel management tab in network settings. Add/verify/edit/delete relays. bUnit tests pass.

tasks.title: Discord Bridge Service (stub)
tasks.prompt:
Create a stub implementation of the Discord Bridge Service (M6.4) — enough structure for future development but not a full implementation.

1. Create Snapp.Service.Discord project with:
   - Program.cs (console app — Discord bots are long-lived, not request/response)
   - IDiscordBotClient interface with methods: ConnectAsync, SendMessageAsync, RegisterSlashCommandsAsync
   - MockDiscordBotClient that logs actions
   - Configuration model for Discord bot token, server mappings
   - Slash command stubs: /snapp link, /snapp benchmark, /snapp profile
   - Account linking endpoint: POST /api/discord/link { discordUserId }

2. Dockerfile + docker-compose entry (commented out — not started by default)

3. README in the service directory explaining how to set up a Discord bot for testing.

This is a stub — the full implementation is deferred until a Discord community is ready to test with.
tasks.done_when: Discord service project exists with interfaces and stubs. Dockerfile ready. README documents setup.

tasks.title: Sprint 15 integration validation
tasks.prompt:
Run exactly these steps. Do not explore, investigate, or retry failures beyond one attempt.

1. `dotnet build snapp.sln` — if it fails, fix the build error and rebuild once.
2. `dotnet test snapp.sln --verbosity quiet` — capture output. Record pass/fail counts per project.
3. Write .claude/reports/sprint-15-validation.md with build result, test table, total counts, any failure names.
4. `git add -A && git commit -m '[S15-004] Sprint 15 integration validation'`

Do NOT re-run failing tests, investigate root causes, fix test code, or do manual walkthroughs.
tasks.done_when: Validation report written with build and test results. Committed.
